code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    df['bb_upper'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_hband(df['close'], window=params['window'], window_dev=params['dev'])
    df['bb_lower'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_lband(df['close'], window=params['window'], window_dev=params['dev'])
    
    # Calculate RSI
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window'])
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype='int')
    
    # Long signal: price touches lower band and RSI < oversold
    signals[(df['low'] <= df['bb_lower']) & (df['rsi'] < params['oversold'])] = 1
    
    # Short signal: price touches upper band and RSI > overbought
    signals[(df['high'] >= df['bb_upper']) & (df['rsi'] > params['overbought'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/JPY exhibits mean-reverting behavior where price tends to reverse after touching Bollinger Bands when confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
